title: GROUP_LEAVE: passing group_id=None to broadcast_to_group is unexplained and inconsistent
description: GROUP_LEAVE calls broadcast_to_group(&remaining_before, ..., None) for group_id, which disables the membership re-check guard (that GROUP_SEND uses). There is no comment explaining why the guard is omitted. A future contributor reading this will either (a) think it's a bug and 'fix' it by adding Some(&group_id) — which would prevent the departed user from receiving their own leave notification, or (b) think GROUP_SEND's guard is unnecessary since GROUP_LEAVE doesn't use it. The code should either explain the intentional asymmetry or (as filed in nie-bqoo.2) this IS a real bug for offline enqueue. In either case a comment is needed.
